title: Dagobah Limited
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}}

|title=''Dagobah Limited''
|game=[[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]<ref name="Decipher">{{Decipher|url=starwars/productlibrary/thecollection/index.html|text=The Expansions}}</ref>

The '''''Dagobah Limited''''' set is the third full expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]], a card game based on the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe. It consists of 180 cards (90 Dark Side and 90 Light Side cards) and was introduced in [[1997]]. This set consists of 80 rare, 50 uncommon, and 50 common cards. This set introduced many of the classic characters from [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] that were involved with the events occurring on [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]], including [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] tests. This set also introduced [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] to game play.

Because this is an expansion set, it bears an expansion-set symbol, which is located near the top right corner of the card to the right of the ''lore'' and below the ''destiny number''. The symbol for this set is [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda's]] green eye.

==Release==
This set was sold in 9-card expansion packs and 60-pack expansion boxes. Each pack included one rare card, three uncommon cards, and five common cards. Starter decks were never produced for this set. The set went out of print in [[December]] of [[1999]] when the [[Dagobah Revised Unlimited]] set came out (an almost identical set with some minor revisions to game text and with white borders instead of black).

==Continuity==
This set was the original source for the names of many characters from Episode V, primarily officers and crew of the ''[[Executor/Legends|Executor]]''. These included [[Venka]], [[Nemet/Legends|Nemet]], [[Hurdiss]], [[Bachenkall]], [[Ardan]] and [[Suba (lieutenant)/Legends|Suba]]. [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Corporal/Legends|corporals]] [[Derdram]] and [[Vandolay]] are identified as members of the ''Executor''{{'s}} crew, but their images actually come from scenes onboard the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Avenger/Legends|Avenger]]''. The character of [[Gherant/Legends|Gherant]] is named in the set though his photo comes from ''Return of the Jedi'', which is the only film he actually appears in.
